Faculty Profile

Dr. Elaine Carter

Senior Fellow, Department of Ethics & Technology. Exploring the intersection of synthetic cognition and moral accountability.

Tenure Since 2014
H-Index 42.8

Research Focus

The Metaphysics of Digital Agency

Dr. Carter’s primary inquiry explores whether autonomous digital systems can possess "moral patiency"—the capacity to be the subject of ethical concern. Her recent work investigates the linguistic bridges between human empathy and algorithmic response patterns.

Key Inquiry 01

Phenomenological reduction in large language models and its implications for subjective experience.

Key Inquiry 02

The "Black Box" Problem: Reconciling transparency requirements with the inherent complexity of neural networks.

Selected Publications

01

Machines That Feel: The Illusion of Sentiment

Published in Journal of Digital Philosophy, Vol. 22 (2023)

Ethics Cognitive Science
02

Architecture of Accountability: Distributed Ledger Ethics

Oxford University Press, 2021

Monograph Legal Frameworks
03

Ghost in the Shell: Algorithmic Bias as Modern Myth

Essay in The Atlantic Scholarly, Dec Issue (2020)

Sociology Tech Policy
